audit_items.proof_trace: All pointer dereferences in getvalues.c are guarded: (1) `entry->lm_ber` at L47/L104 cannot be NULL — in result.c::try_read1msg, `lm_ber` is assigned from `lc->lconn_ber` which is explicitly allocated and validated (result.c:477-486) before `newmsg->lm_ber = ber` (result.c:997); (2) `attr` from ber_scanf('a') is always used only after a successful return, since LBER_DEFAULT==LBER_ERROR and ber_scanf's error cleanup sets `*ss=NULL` before returning LBER_ERROR; (3) helper functions ldap_count_values, ldap_value_free, ldap_value_free_len, and ldap_value_dup each explicitly check or delegate to NULL-safe routines (ber_memvfree_x/ber_bvecfree_x both return immediately on NULL input).
